punch: An app built for inconsistent income.
caption_ig:
It's here.

Able is live in the App Store.

If you're a freelancer, creator, contractor, or 1099 worker whose paycheck doesn't show up the same day every month, this app was built for you.

Most budgeting apps assume a steady paycheck. Yours isn't one. So the math doesn't work, the leaks happen, and by the 30th the money is gone.

Able is different. Every deposit gets a purpose the moment it lands.

Bills covered. Tax set aside. Reserve growing. Nothing left to leak.

30 days free. Live one full income cycle. Then decide.

Link in bio.

caption_li:
Able is now live in the App Store.

It's a budgeting app built specifically for inconsistent income, not monthly paychecks.

If you're a freelancer, creator, contractor, or 1099 worker, the math you need is different. Monthly budgets assume your paycheck shows up the same day every month. Yours doesn't.

Able's Floor-First method gives every deposit a purpose the moment it lands. Taxes covered. Bills covered. Then debt, reserve, and free spending in that order.

30 days free. Live one full income cycle, then decide.

App Store link in the first comment.

caption_li:
The reason most freelancers and 1099 workers feel stuck with money isn't a discipline problem.

It's a tool problem.

Every budgeting app on the market assumes a steady paycheck. Same amount. Same day. Every month.

If your income doesn't arrive that way, the budget breaks the first time a deposit is late or smaller than expected. And then the leaks start.

You're not bad with money. The advice was never built for you.

Able is the first budgeting app built specifically for inconsistent income.

30 days free. Live one full income cycle.

App Store link in the first comment.

punch: Built for the fear of the next check.
caption_ig:
Freelancers don't fail to pay down debt because they don't want to.

They fail because they're afraid of not knowing when the next check is coming.

So the cash sits. Just in case. The debt grows. The savings stall.

It isn't a willpower problem. It's a structural one.

Able solves it. Reserve takes the weight off 'what if the next check is late.' Floor stays covered. Debt actually moves.

30 days free. App Store. Link in bio.

caption_li:
The single insight that shaped how Able got built:

Freelancers and 1099 workers don't fail to pay down debt because they don't want to. They fail because they're afraid of not knowing when the next check is coming.

So the cash sits in checking. Just in case.

The debt grows. The savings stall. The shame compounds.

It isn't a willpower problem. It's a structural one. The solution isn't a stricter monthly budget. It's a reserve that absorbs gap weeks so the floor never falls.

Able is built around that mechanic.

Floor-First Budgeting. Five rules.

1. Know your floor. Bills + tax. The unmissable amount.

2. Every deposit fills the floor first.

3. Build your reserve before you spend on anything else.

4. One month ahead is the goal. Not perfection. Cycle.

5. Score reality, not the plan.

That's the method. Able is the tool.

caption_li:
Floor-First Budgeting in 5 rules:

1. Know your floor. Bills + tax. The unmissable amount every cycle has to cover.

2. Every deposit fills the floor first. Before debt. Before savings. Before anything else.

3. Build your reserve before you spend. One month of floor in the bank is the target.

4. One month ahead is the goal. Not perfection. Cycle.

5. Score reality, not the plan. The cycle that just happened beats the budget you wrote.

That's the methodology. Able is the tool that runs it for you.

caption_ig:
Three steps to a budget that survives inconsistent income.

1. Set your floor. Bills + tax. The minimum every cycle has to cover.

2. Route every deposit. Taxes off the top. Bills next. Then the rest.

3. Build a reserve. One month ahead. So a slow week doesn't break the plan.

That's it. No spreadsheet. No envelope ritual. No monthly budget that falls apart when a deposit is late.

Able runs this for you. 30 days free in the App Store. Link in bio.
